meta.title: Advanced Rhetoric & Stylistics
meta.description: Analyse rhetorical strategies, stylistic choices, and their effects on audiences.
meta.description_ko: 수사적 전략, 문체적 선택, 청중에 대한 효과를 분석하는 방법을 학습합니다.

explanation.en: 'Synecdoche' = a part represents the whole (e.g., 'boots on the ground' = soldiers).
explanation.ko: 'synecdoche' = 부분이 전체를 나타냄 (예: 'boots on the ground' = 군인들).

choices: 전체를 대신하는 부분, 또는 부분을 대신하는 전체 (비유)

explanation.en: 'Anaphora' = rhetorical repetition of words/phrases at the beginning of clauses.
explanation.ko: 'anaphora' = 절의 시작 부분에서 단어/구의 수사적 반복.

questions.prompt: The speech employed _____ — the same phrase repeated at the beginning of successive clauses — to powerful effect.

explanation.en: Chiasmus: 'Ask not what your country can do for you — ask what you can do for your country.' (A-B, B-A structure).
explanation.ko: chiasmus: 'Ask not what your country can do for you — ask what you can do for your country.' (A-B, B-A 구조).

choices: I came, I saw, I conquered.
Ask not what your country can do for you — ask what you can do for your country.
To be or not to be.
We have nothing to fear but fear itself.

explanation.en: 'Pathos' = emotional appeal; one of Aristotle's three modes of persuasion.
explanation.ko: 'pathos' = 감정적 호소; 아리스토텔레스의 세 가지 설득 방식 중 하나.

questions.answer: The speaker's use of pathos appeals directly to the audience's emotions, bypassing rational analysis.

explanation.en: 'The pen is mightier than the sword' = metonymy: pen=writing/intellect, sword=military force.
explanation.ko: 'The pen is mightier than the sword' = metonymy: 펜=글쓰기/지성, 검=군사력.

choices: The pen is mightier than the sword

explanation.en: 'Epiphora' = repetition at the END of clauses (opposite of anaphora).
explanation.ko: 'epiphora' = 절의 끝 부분에서의 반복 (anaphora의 반대).

questions.prompt: The repetition of the final words at the end of successive clauses is called _____.

explanation.en: 'Litotes' = understatement using double negative: 'not unkind' = quite kind.
explanation.ko: 'litotes' = 이중 부정을 사용한 절제된 표현: 'not unkind' = 꽤 친절한.

explanation.en: 'I Have a Dream' = iconic anaphora, repeating the phrase to build emotional momentum.
explanation.ko: 'I Have a Dream' = 아이코닉한 anaphora, 구절을 반복하여 감정적 모멘텀을 구축.

questions.prompt: Martin Luther King's 'I Have a Dream' speech is celebrated for its masterful use of _____, creating a powerful cumulative effect through repetition.

explanation.en: 'Ethos' = appeal based on the speaker's credibility, authority, or character.
explanation.ko: 'ethos' = 화자의 신뢰성, 권위, 성격에 기반한 호소.

questions.prompt: 'ethos'는 아리스토텔레스의 3대 설득 방식 중 무엇에 해당하나요?

explanation.en: 'Tone' = the emotional quality or attitude conveyed by the writing.
explanation.ko: 'tone' = 글이 전달하는 감정적 특성이나 태도.

questions.prompt: The _____ of the passage — its overall emotional quality — shifts dramatically after the introduction of the child's perspective.

explanation.en: 'Zeugma' = one word (usually a verb) governs two or more phrases with different meanings.
explanation.ko: 'zeugma' = 하나의 단어(보통 동사)가 서로 다른 의미를 가진 두 개 이상의 구를 지배.

choices: 하나의 단어가 두 개 이상의 다른 의미로 절을 지배함

explanation.en: 'Antithesis' = juxtaposition of contrasting ideas for rhetorical effect.
explanation.ko: 'antithesis' = 수사적 효과를 위한 대조적 아이디어의 병치.

questions.answer: The author's use of antithesis sharpens the contrast between freedom and oppression.

explanation.en: 'Bottom-up' or inductive reasoning: specific → general conclusion.
explanation.ko: 'bottom-up' 또는 귀납적 추론: 특정 → 일반 결론.

questions.prompt: The _____ structure of the argument — moving from specific examples to a general conclusion — is typical of inductive reasoning.

explanation.en: Syllogism: two premises leading logically to a conclusion. Classic form of deductive reasoning.
explanation.ko: 삼단논법: 두 전제에서 논리적으로 결론 도출. 연역적 추론의 고전적 형태.

choices: All men are mortal. Socrates is a man. Therefore, Socrates is mortal.
The more I know, the less I understand.
Ask not what your country can do for you.

explanation.en: 'Platitudes' = trite or overused remarks that sound meaningful but say little.
explanation.ko: 'platitudes' = 의미 있게 들리지만 실질적인 내용이 거의 없는 진부한 발언.

questions.prompt: The politician's speech was full of _____ — vague, feel-good language that avoids specific commitments.

explanation.en: 'Anadiplosis' = repeating the last word of one clause at the start of the next.
explanation.ko: 'anadiplosis' = 한 절의 마지막 단어를 다음 절의 시작에 반복.

choices: 한 절의 마지막 단어를 다음 절의 시작에 반복

explanation.en: Rhetoric = purposeful use of language to persuade and move audiences.
explanation.ko: 수사학 = 청중을 설득하고 움직이기 위한 의도적인 언어 사용.

questions.answer: Effective rhetoric does not merely inform but shapes perception and moves its audience to action.

explanation.en: 'Apostrophe' (rhetorical) = addressing someone/something absent or abstract.
explanation.ko: 'apostrophe'(수사) = 부재하거나 추상적인 사람/것에 말 걸기.

questions.prompt: The writer employs _____ — addressing an absent or imaginary person — to create intimacy and dramatic effect.

explanation.en: 'Isocolon' = a succession of clauses of the same length and structure.
explanation.ko: 'isocolon' = 같은 길이와 구조의 절들의 연속.

choices: 문장 길이가 같은 연속 절들로 이루어진 수사 기법

explanation.en: 'Tricolon' = a series of three parallel elements: 'I came, I saw, I conquered'.
explanation.ko: 'tricolon' = 세 가지 평행 요소의 연속: 'I came, I saw, I conquered'.

questions.answer: The orator's use of tricolon created a memorable and rhythmically satisfying conclusion.

explanation.en: 'Rhetorical' (adjective) + 'effects' (noun). 'Rhetoric affects' mixes noun + verb incorrectly.
explanation.ko: 'rhetorical'(형용사) + 'effects'(명사). 'rhetoric affects'는 명사 + 동사를 잘못 혼합.

questions.prompt: 틀린 부분을 찾으세요: 'The speech's rhetoric affects were remarkable given the hostile audience.'

explanation.en: 'Peroration' = the concluding section of a speech, often the most emotionally charged.
explanation.ko: 'peroration' = 연설의 마지막 부분, 종종 감정적으로 가장 강렬한.

questions.prompt: Her closing _____ — 'We shall fight, we shall never surrender' — exemplifies the power of anaphora.

explanation.en: 'Chiasmus' = crossing/reversal structure (A-B, B-A).
explanation.ko: 'chiasmus' = 교차/역전 구조 (A-B, B-A).

questions.prompt: 빈칸에 알맞은 것은? 'A: The speech had a powerful 'A-B, B-A' structure. What's that called? B: That's _____ — it reverses the grammatical order of repeated phrases.'

explanation.en: Core rhetorical devices and their definitions.
explanation.ko: 핵심 수사 기법과 정의.
